title: Commit barrel references untracked modules (amount.ts / ingest.ts)
description: Commit b29eeaa wires roundMoney through src/features/ledger/index.ts, which also re-exports ./lib/amount and ./lib/ingest. Those two modules are still untracked in the working tree (uncommitted ADR 0001 / ingest work). A clean checkout of b29eeaa would fail to compile. I did not pull them into this commit because they fall outside the ADR 0002 work order's scope. A human should decide how to land the prerequisite modules.

title: Build-integrity gate: b29eeaa barrel references untracked amount.ts / ingest.ts
description: Commit b29eeaa (ADR 0002) added src/features/ledger/index.ts exports from ./lib/amount and ./lib/ingest, but neither file has ever been committed (git log empty; both untracked). A clean checkout of b29eeaa fails tsc and module resolution. The prerequisite modules must land before this is safe on main. This is the sole blocker — the round.ts logic itself is sound.

title: Dependency CVE H1: postcss <8.5.10 XSS — apply override or accept risk
description: npm audit flags a known XSS CVE in the postcss bundled transitively under next@16.2.6. The only npm-suggested 'fix' is a semver-major downgrade of next to 9.3.3, which must NOT be applied. Recommended remediation is a package.json overrides pin of postcss>=8.5.10. Reachability is low (build-time CSS processing, mock-data app), advisory severity is moderate. Decide how to remediate.

title: Publish v0.3.0 tag — clear build-integrity gate first
description: Version bump (0.2.0→0.3.0), changelog, and release notes for ai-bookkeeper v0.3.0 are prepared. The annotated git tag was NOT created because the committed barrel src/features/ledger/index.ts re-exports ./lib/amount and ./lib/ingest, but amount.ts/amount.test.ts/ingest.ts are untracked at HEAD (b29eeaa) — tagging now would publish a release whose source imports files absent from git history. Decide how to proceed with the tag.
